QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_72

question.text: Co-location (or proximity hosting) facility offered by stock exchanges is primarily used to:

answer.text: Minimize trading latency by placing systems within or close to exchange premises
Provide office space for brokers to meet clients
Train new brokers on exchange systems

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Co-location allows stock brokers and data vendors to locate their trading/data-vending systems within or at close proximity to exchange premises, connecting to the trading platform through direct and private networks. This minimizes latency for faster execution. **The Trap:** SEBI mandates fair and equitable access to co-location facilities — no preferential treatment to any particular broker. This prevents unfair advantages. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers co-location in the context of algo/HFT trading. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Co-location = speed advantage (microseconds matter in HFT). But SEBI ensures level playing field: equal access, fair pricing, no discrimination. Co-location is just one way to connect — others include leased line, VSAT, internet.
